Patch Set 1:

You might consider to add "trivial" to this patch through to the later ones that already have it.  It is the 3rd *build* in the test queue, but that is still 45 test sessions from the front (about 1/5 of the test cluster), so the ~1-2h spent (unfortunately) rebuilding the patch will still probably mean that this and all later patches will finish testing sooner.

While it is possible to cancel test sessions after they have been submitted or started, the lack of a "pass" for those sessions will prevent the patch from getting Maloo +1 needed for landing.

If a new build comes in for an existing patch, then all of the old sessions will be cancelled automatically (whether running or queued) and the Maloo scorecard will be reset for the new build.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

parents.subject: LU-18231 obd: change obd_abort_recovery into OBDF_ABORT_RECOVERY

commit.subject: LU-18231 obd: change obd_abort_mdt_recovery into OBDF_ABORT_MDT_RECOVERY
commit.message:
LU-18231 obd: change obd_abort_mdt_recovery into OBDF_ABORT_MDT_RECOVERY

Change the use of the odb_abort_mdt_recovery bitfield into a bit in
the ofd_flags bitmap and use atomic bit set/clear/test
operations on it.
No logical changes to the code and no changes to locking.

Test-Parameters: trivial
